symbols: palm branches — martyrdom and victory over death through faith
chalice held by Saint Barbara — her role as patroness of a holy death and the Eucharist
wheel/wheel fragment held by Saint Catherine — her legendary martyrdom on a spiked wheel
elaborate royal garments — both saints' noble birth and their heavenly queenship
gold background — the divine light of heaven in which the saints now dwell
description: An Orthodox icon depicting two female saints standing side by side against a gold background. The figure on the left (Saint Barbara) wears a red dress under a richly patterned teal-green and gold mantle, and holds a palm branch and a golden chalice. The figure on the right (Saint Catherine) wears a green-and-gold bodice under an ornate orange-red mantle with elaborate floral patterns, and holds a palm branch and what appears to be a wheel or cross. Both women wear crowns or decorative headpieces and have serene, youthful faces with halos.
theological_description: Saints Barbara and Catherine of Alexandria are among the most venerated of the early Christian virgin martyrs and are frequently depicted together as models of female courage and faith. Saint Barbara, according to tradition, was imprisoned in a tower by her pagan father and subsequently martyred for her conversion to Christianity. She is invoked as patroness of a holy death, and the chalice she holds symbolizes the Eucharistic grace she prayed to receive before her execution. Saint Catherine of Alexandria, legendarily a woman of great learning, debated and defeated fifty pagan philosophers before being condemned to die on a spiked wheel — which miraculously shattered — and was then beheaded. Together they embody the twofold Christian witness: Barbara represents the hidden, interior faith that persists under persecution within the domestic sphere, while Catherine represents the public, intellectual defense of the faith against pagan philosophy. Their joint veneration affirms that holiness is not limited by gender and that the blood of the martyrs is the seed of the Church.

scripture_refs.reason: Those who have come out of the great tribulation and washed their robes in the blood of the Lamb — the heavenly state of all martyrs
Be faithful unto death and I will give you the crown of life — the promise both saints fulfilled
Always be prepared to give a reason for the hope that is in you — embodied in Catherine's defense of faith before the philosophers
Do not fear those who kill the body but cannot kill the soul — the courage both saints demonstrated
The crown of righteousness laid up for those who have fought the good fight — symbolized by their crowns and palms
